description: Opening streams, connections, or files without try-with-resources, leaking handles when an exception skips close().
body: Failure mode: a stream or JDBC connection is closed in normal flow but an exception before close() leaks the handle; under load file descriptors or DB connections exhaust. Detection signals: 'Too many open files' or connection-pool-timeout errors over time; descriptor count grows in monitoring; close() is in a code path an exception can bypass. Do not manage closeable resources with manual close in plain try/finally that misses paths. Safer fallback: use try-with-resources (try (var c = open()) { ... }) so AutoCloseable.close runs on every exit, and verify descriptor/connection counts stay flat under sustained load.
